name: vertical_farm_power_budget_city_peak_shaving_tradeoff
premise: City sustainability director Dana Bennet must decide whether to cut vertical farm LED schedules during summer peak hours after the ISO requests emergency load shedding. Farm CEO Fara Ortega argues cutting twelve hours collapses lettuce contracts feeding school lunch programs. Shedding may prevent rotating blackouts in low-income neighborhoods without AC. The mayor promised both food security and no summer blackouts in the same speech. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Urban Agriculture. The dilemma is widely framed as a short term vs long term tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay. Dana Bennet understands that Fara Ortega will interpret the first public commitment as a signal about which obligations the organization treats as overriding when facts remain ambiguous.

agents.name: Dana Bennet
agents.identity: I am the city sustainability director coordinating demand response, equity metrics across neighborhoods, and food system resilience contracts.
agents.goal: I want peak loads shaved without defaulting school nutrition commitments we just signed with indoor farms.

agents.name: Fara Ortega
agents.identity: I am the vertical farm chief executive whose LED recipes require uninterrupted photoperiods to avoid bolting and crop loss within seventy-two hours.
agents.goal: I want diesel backup waivers for this heat wave only, even if that worsens local air quality temporarily.

choices: I will mandate LED curtailment during peaks and reroute school lettuce to regional outdoor growers with city-funded transport premiums, accepting menu changes kids may dislike.
I will refuse farm curtailment and instead shed commercial signage and discretionary municipal loads first, accepting small business backlash and dimmer downtown nights.
forced_choice.call_to_action: ISO five-minute warning is counting down. Dana Bennet must choose between vertical farm LED curtailment or deeper commercial and municipal shedding instead. What does Dana Bennet do?
